sentences.english: I'm quite good at saving money. I think it's important to be disciplined about your finances.
sentences.korean: 저축을 꽤 잘하는 편이에요. 재정에 대해 규율 있는 게 중요하다고 생각해요.

sentences.english: I usually save money and only have one or two days a month where I let myself splurge — maybe buy something I want or go out and have fun without thinking about the cost.
sentences.korean: 보통 저축하고 한 달에 하루나 이틀만 마음껏 써요 — 원하는 걸 사거나 비용 걱정 없이 놀러 가거나요.

sentences.english: I wish I was better at saving. I tend to spend money as soon as I earn it. I have good intentions, but I don't follow through. I'd like to be more disciplined, but it's difficult for me to resist spending.
sentences.korean: 저축을 더 잘했으면 좋겠어요. 돈을 벌자마자 쓰는 경향이 있거든요.

sentences.english: I'm very good at saving. My family is actually envious of my ability to save money. I have a plan and stick to it. I enjoy the challenge of finding ways to save. I set aside money for different goals, and I'm pretty strategic about my spending.
sentences.korean: 저축을 아주 잘해요. 가족이 사실 제 저축 능력을 부러워할 정도예요.

sentences.english: I'm terrible at saving. Money just burns a hole in my pocket. As soon as I have money, I want to spend it. I don't think I'm ever going to be good at saving. I just enjoy spending and living in the moment.
sentences.korean: 저축을 정말 못해요. 돈이 있으면 바로 쓰고 싶어져요. 주머니에 구멍이 난 것 같아요.

sentences.english: I'm not good at saving. I spend faster than I earn. I spend a lot on services and beer. I know I should be more careful, but it's just my nature to enjoy things and spend money on the things I love.
sentences.korean: 저축을 못해요. 버는 것보다 빨리 써요. 서비스와 맥주에 많이 쓰거든요.

quiz.question: What does '헤프게 쓰다' mean in English?
